toolSelection.reason: Raw traces show a stable shared-runtime failure class where named targets do not resolve deterministically into the right grounded path; this is visible in the wrong-path and wrong-event traces, plus one zero-grounded-call miss.

scoutDepth.reason: The analyzer penalizes this dimension broadly, but the raw traces usually had enough evidence to execute; the low signal is more calibration-heavy than failure-heavy.

clarification.reason: There is not enough adverse signal here; nearly all prompts had a single grounded interpretation and did not fail because clarification was missing.

planning.reason: Multiple traces show the same planner-level issue: overly flexible candidate handling or validation complexity before execution, even when the final answer eventually lands.

execution.reason: The retry/self-heal pattern is stable across several traces, including one bounded-budget stop after the runtime picked an imperfect path.

synthesis.reason: This is the broadest recurring weakness: grounded data often reached the answer path, but the final response still under-delivered on differentiation, completeness, or buyer-facing actionability.

contextBudget.reason: Most traces had enough raw evidence; lower-scoring cases were usually caused by wrong-target resolution or partial execution, not true context starvation.

reasoningDepth.reason: The signal is coupled to selection and planning failures rather than standing alone as a clean post-experiment bottleneck.

mcpTransport.reason: The post-experiment traces do not show a stable transport-layer failure cluster; misses were primarily upstream of transport or downstream in synthesis.

codeSafety.reason: Planner/runtime code validation remains an actionable shared-runtime concern, including a severe failure where a disallowed helper import prevented any grounded execution.

synthesisBudget.reason: The runtime often had enough facts, but the final answer budget was not consistently spent on discriminative comparisons, explicit ranking logic, or closing known gaps.

interactionRisks.risk: When entity resolution is soft, the planner can lock onto the wrong event or insufficient target and then produce a coherent but misgrounded program.
Over-complex or insufficiently validated plans create retries, bounded-budget exits, or code-generation failures before the runtime reaches a stable grounded answer.
Partial or weakly recovered execution results can still be surfaced as complete-looking answers, which hides residual uncertainty and reduces differentiation.

summary.why: The most stable post-experiment runtime failure is not lack of grounding but failure to convert grounded data into clearly differentiated final answers; 10 of 18 traces fit this pattern, which is broader than the sharper but less frequent selection misses.
